flags.concern: media-lightbox-persistence: phase 3 overstates what can be deleted from the autosave machinery. The repo still sends other text fields through the same per-keystroke path, and `hasPendingFor()` is also the load-time guard that prevents async loads from overwriting local edits. If the executor removes the edit-version and loader-side pending logic after changing only the main prompt flow, fast edits in the remaining text inputs or edits made before load resolves can still be lost.
flags.evidence: Revised Phase 3 scope per FLAG-001: step 10 now covers every free-text field (Img2Img prompt, custom LoRA URL, plus any others found by grep), not just the main prompt, and explicitly preserves `pendingSettingsRef` / `hasPendingFor` / the loader-side guards at `autoSaveSettingsLoaders.ts:61,132` — only the per-save edit-version comparator is removed. Revised Phase 2 step 7 per FLAG-002: `EditModePanel` consumes three contexts (form + lightbox-core + variants), and `InlineEditView` currently depends on `stateOverrides.coreState.onClose`. The step now mandates enumerating the full override surface, compares wrapping-in-providers (Path A) vs pure-props (Path B), and picks the smaller diff at implementation time instead of pre-committing to Path A. Success criteria updated to reflect broader prompt-persistence scope and the preserved loader-race logic.

flags.concern: edit-panel-state-ownership: phase 2 step 7's preferred "wrap `InlineEditView` in a real `ImageEditProvider` and delete `stateOverrides`" is incomplete for this repo. `EditModePanel` does not only consume image-edit form state; it also falls back to `useLightboxCoreSafe()` and `useLightboxVariantsSafe()`. `InlineEditView` lives outside `LightboxStateProvider` and currently relies on `stateOverrides.coreState` to supply a real close handler, so deleting the override layer without adding the matching lightbox providers would regress inline-edit controls back to safe no-op defaults.

The step now mandates enumerating the full override surface, compares wrapping-in-providers (Path A) vs pure-props (Path B), and picks the smaller diff at implementation time instead of pre-committing to Path A.
